steps.title: Identify the time signal
steps.content: The phrase "nearly 150 years ago" is a specific past-time adverb. It pins Van Gogh's completion of the drawing to a finished moment in the past. Only a tense that expresses a completed past action is compatible with this adverb — present perfect, present progressive, and future tenses are all ruled out by it.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — has been completing: present perfect progressive. Present perfect cannot combine with a specific past-time adverb like "years ago," and progressive aspect implies the action is still ongoing — contradicts "nearly 150 years ago." ✗
  • Choice B — is completing: present progressive. Describes an action happening right now, which directly contradicts "150 years ago." ✗
  • Choice C — will complete: simple future. Refers to something that has not yet happened — impossible for an event 150 years in the past. ✗
  • Choice D — completed: simple past. The only tense that correctly expresses a finished action at a specific past point in time. ✓
D is correct.
